meta.title: NetWalking | Tokyo's 5km Walking Community
meta.description: Hate networking events? Same. So we walk instead: 5km through Tokyo, once a month. No pitches. No RSVPs. Free.
meta.ogDescription: Hate networking events? Same. So we walk instead: 5km through Tokyo, once a month.

subLines: So we walk instead. 5km through Tokyo, once a month.
hero.small: Built by a dad who'd rather be home for dinner.

specs.note: At the end, we donate ┬Ц100 per km walked to charity. Totally optional.

language.heading: Zero Japanese? You're in.
lines: English OK. Japanese OK. Mixing both is the house style.
The more nervous you are, the warmer the welcome.
Some of the best conversations here happen in broken everything.

steps.title: Show up
steps.desc: No RSVP. The first 20-30 minutes is buffer time, so being late is fine.

steps.title: Walk and talk
steps.desc: About 2 hours, around 5km. We shuffle groups along the way.

steps.title: Intros and contacts
steps.desc: Mid-walk intro circle: your name, your time in Japan, what to call you. Swap contacts at the end.

why.heading: Why we walk at lunch.
paragraphs: I'm an entrepreneur, but I'm a dad first. I want dinner with my kids every night. So I don't do evening networking.
I built the open-air lunchtime version instead. That's NetWalking.

footer.arrival: That's 5km. Now walk the real one.
footer.tagline: One step at a time, stronger together.

playbook.title: NetWalking: How To Run Your Own
